annotations.label: Presheaf of sets

anchor.snippet: A presheaf

annotations.note: `TopCat.Presheaf C X` is a contravariant functor from open sets of `X` to `C`, instantiated for `C = Type*` gives presheaves of sets.

annotations.label: Presheaf of continuous functions

anchor.snippet: Many examples of presheaves come from different classes of functions

annotations.note: `presheafToTop X T` is the presheaf of continuous maps from open subsets of `X` to a topological space `T`.

annotations.label: Constant presheaf

anchor.snippet: Another common class of examples is assigning

annotations.note: The constant presheaf is built via `(Functor.const _).obj A`; there is no dedicated `constantPresheaf` name.

annotations.label: Sheaf (locality and gluing)

anchor.snippet: Axiomatically, a sheaf is a presheaf that satisfies both of the following axioms

annotations.note: `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf` and the bundled `TopCat.Sheaf C X` express the locality + gluing axioms (equivalent formulation: `Presheaf.IsSheafUniqueGluing`).

annotations.label: Separated presheaf / monopresheaf

anchor.snippet: A separated presheaf , or monopresheaf , is a presheaf satisfying axiom 1

annotations.note: `Presheaf.IsSeparated` (and `Presieve.IsSeparatedFor` for individual sieves) captures the uniqueness-of-gluing axiom alone.

annotations.label: Continuous functions form a sheaf

anchor.snippet: The presheaf consisting of continuous functions mentioned above is a sheaf

annotations.note: `TopCat.sheafToTop` packages continuous functions into a topological space as a sheaf of types.

annotations.label: Quasi-coherent sheaf on Spec(A)

anchor.snippet: This observation is used to construct another example which is crucial in algebraic geometry, namely quasi-coherent sheaves

annotations.note: The structure sheaf on `Spec R` (and module-valued variant for an `R`-module `M`) is defined; the general notion `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` also exists.

annotations.label: Fibre-product characterization of sheaves

anchor.snippet: There is another characterization of sheaves that is equivalent to the previously discussed

annotations.note: `isSheaf_iff_isSheafPairwiseIntersections` proves the sheaf condition is equivalent to the pullback (pairwise intersections) characterization.

annotations.label: Sheaf of sections of a continuous map

anchor.section: Sheaf of sections of a continuous map
anchor.snippet: Any continuous map

annotations.note: The local predicate `isSection p` produces the sheaf of sections of `p : T → X` via `subsheafToTypes`, but the construction is only set up for trivial fiber families and not the general `Y → X` étalé construction.

annotations.label: Sheaf of branches of complex logarithm

anchor.section: Sheaf of sections of a continuous map
anchor.snippet: is the sheaf which assigns to any

annotations.note: No declaration of the sheaf of branches of the complex logarithm is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Skyscraper sheaf

anchor.section: Sheaf of sections of a continuous map
anchor.snippet: the skyscraper sheaf

annotations.note: `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` and its sheaf upgrade `skyscraperSheaf` formalize the skyscraper sheaf at a point.

annotations.label: Structure sheaf and differential forms on manifolds

anchor.snippet: there are a number of important sheaves, such as the sheaf

annotations.note: The structure sheaf of smooth functions on a manifold is formalized as `smoothSheafCommRing`, but differential-forms sheaves are not assembled as sheaves in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cosheaf of compactly supported functions; sheaf of distributions

anchor.snippet: The assignment sending

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain a cosheaf of compactly supported functions nor a sheaf of distributions.

annotations.label: Two-point space presheaf (not separated)

anchor.snippet: be the two-point topological space

annotations.note: This counterexample of a non-separated presheaf on the two-point space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bounded continuous functions (not a sheaf)

anchor.snippet: be the set of bounded continuous functions

annotations.note: The example showing the presheaf of bounded continuous functions fails the gluing axiom is not formalized.

annotations.label: Morphism of sheaves

anchor.snippet: A morphism

annotations.note: Morphisms of sheaves arise via the category instance on `Sheaf J A` (full subcategory of presheaves), so a sheaf morphism is a natural transformation of underlying functors.

annotations.label: Derivative as morphism of sheaves

anchor.snippet: For example, taking the derivative gives a morphism of sheaves

annotations.note: The derivative is not formalized as a morphism between sheaves of differentiable functions.

annotations.label: Iso/monomorphism check on open sets

anchor.snippet: of sheaves on

annotations.note: `isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` and `mono_iff_stalk_mono` reduce iso/mono-ness of a sheaf morphism to stalkwise checks.

annotations.label: Stalk of a sheaf

anchor.snippet: the stalk is defined by

annotations.note: `TopCat.Presheaf.stalk F x` is the colimit over open neighborhoods, exactly the stalk.

annotations.label: Germ at a point

anchor.snippet: takes a section

annotations.note: `TopCat.Presheaf.germ F U x hx` is the canonical map sending a section on `U` to its germ in the stalk at `x`.

annotations.label: Sheafification

anchor.section: Turning a presheaf into a sheaf
anchor.snippet: called the sheafification or sheaf associated to the presheaf

annotations.note: `sheafify J P` (with adjunction unit `toSheafify`) and the functor `presheafToSheaf` realize the sheaf associated to a presheaf.

annotations.label: Universal property of sheafification

anchor.section: Turning a presheaf into a sheaf
anchor.snippet: is the best possible approximation to

annotations.note: `sheafificationAdjunction` is the adjunction `presheafToSheaf ⊣ sheafToPresheaf`, expressing the universal property of sheafification.

annotations.label: Quotient sheaf

anchor.snippet: the quotient sheaf

annotations.note: Subsheaves are formalized via `Subfunctor`/`Subpresheaf`; the abelian-category quotient sheaf of a subsheaf is obtained via colimits/sheafification but not under a dedicated `quotientSheaf` name.

annotations.label: Sheaf hom

anchor.snippet: The sheaf hom of

annotations.note: `sheafHom F G` (with underlying `presheafHom`) is the internal-hom sheaf of sheaves of types.

annotations.label: Direct sum and tensor product of sheaves

anchor.snippet: The direct sum of

annotations.note: The category `SheafOfModules R` is preadditive (so direct sums exist) but a sheaf-of-modules tensor product is not provided as a dedicated declaration.

annotations.label: Direct image (pushforward) of a sheaf

anchor.snippet: The pushforward (also known as direct image )

annotations.note: `TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward C f` (with sheaf version `TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward`) is the direct image along a continuous map.

annotations.label: Direct image with compact support

anchor.snippet: the direct image with compact support is a subsheaf of the direct image

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the direct image with proper/compact support `f_!`.

annotations.label: Inverse image (pullback) of a sheaf

anchor.snippet: The pullback or inverse image goes the other way

annotations.note: `TopCat.Sheaf.pullback f` (with `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`) is the inverse-image functor on sheaves over topological spaces.

annotations.label: Locally constant sheaf

anchor.snippet: is called locally constant

annotations.note: No declaration of a locally constant sheaf was found in Mathlib (only `LocallyConstant` for maps).

annotations.label: Extension by zero

anchor.snippet: the extension by zero

annotations.note: The extension-by-zero functor `j_!` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: C-valued presheaf

anchor.snippet: Presheaves with values in an arbitrary category

annotations.note: `TopCat.Presheaf C X := (Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ C` is the general definition of presheaves valued in any category `C`.

annotations.label: Sheaf condition via equalizer

anchor.snippet: admits all limits

annotations.note: `Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isLimit` (and `isSheaf_iff_multifork`, `isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts`) express the sheaf condition as a limit/equalizer condition.

annotations.label: Ringed space

anchor.section: Ringed spaces and sheaves of modules
anchor.snippet: is called a ringed space

annotations.note: `RingedSpace` is defined as a `SheafedSpace CommRingCat`.

annotations.label: Locally ringed space

anchor.section: Ringed spaces and sheaves of modules
anchor.snippet: are local rings , in which case the pair is called a locally ringed space

annotations.note: `LocallyRingedSpace` extends `SheafedSpace CommRingCat` with the condition that all stalks are local rings.

annotations.label: Sheaf of modules

anchor.section: Ringed spaces and sheaves of modules
anchor.snippet: a sheaf of modules is a sheaf

annotations.note: `SheafOfModules R` is defined for a sheaf of rings `R : Sheaf J RingCat` on a site.

annotations.label: Vector bundles as locally free sheaves

anchor.section: Ringed spaces and sheaves of modules
anchor.snippet: there is a one-to-one correspondence between vector bundles and locally free sheaves

annotations.note: Mathlib has vector bundles and `SheafOfModules`, but no formalized equivalence between vector bundles and locally free sheaves.

annotations.label: Finitely generated and finitely presented sheaves

anchor.section: Finiteness conditions for sheaves of modules
anchor.snippet: is called finitely generated

annotations.note: `SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType` captures the finitely generated condition; `QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation` covers finite presentation in the quasicoherent setting.

annotations.label: Coherent sheaf

anchor.section: Finiteness conditions for sheaves of modules
anchor.snippet: is called a coherent sheaf if it is of finite type

annotations.note: Mathlib's `coherentTopology`/`CoherentSheaves` files are about the coherent Grothendieck topology, not coherent sheaves of modules in the Serre/EGA sense.

annotations.label: Oka coherence theorem

anchor.section: Finiteness conditions for sheaves of modules
anchor.snippet: The Oka coherence theorem states that the sheaf of holomorphic functions on a complex manifold is coherent

annotations.note: The Oka coherence theorem (coherence of the sheaf of holomorphic functions) is not present; `RingTheory/Ideal/Oka.lean` is unrelated (Oka ideals).

annotations.label: Étalé space of a sheaf

anchor.section: The étalé space of a sheaf
anchor.snippet: the étalé space (sometimes called the étale space )

annotations.note: No étalé/espace étalé construction is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence: sheaves of sets and étalé spaces

anchor.section: The étalé space of a sheaf
anchor.snippet: The construction above determines an equivalence of categories

annotations.note: The equivalence between sheaves of sets and étalé spaces over `X` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Long exact sequence in sheaf cohomology

anchor.snippet: there is a long exact sequence

annotations.note: Sheaf cohomology groups `Sheaf.H F n` are defined, but the long exact sequence associated to a short exact sequence of sheaves is not stated as a named theorem.

annotations.label: De Rham complex resolves the constant sheaf

anchor.snippet: the de Rham complex is a resolution of the constant sheaf

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the de Rham complex as a resolution of the constant sheaf on a manifold.

annotations.label: Perverse sheaves

anchor.snippet: Perverse sheaves are certain objects in

annotations.note: No definition of perverse sheaves exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Site

anchor.snippet: A category with a Grothendieck topology is called a site

annotations.note: A site is realized as a pair `(C, J : GrothendieckTopology C)`; Mathlib also has `Coverage`/`Pretopology` formalizations.

annotations.label: Topos / Grothendieck topos

anchor.snippet: A category of sheaves on a site is called a topos or a Grothendieck topos

annotations.note: The category `Sheaf J A` of sheaves on a site exists (and `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Topos.Sheaf` discusses it as an elementary topos), but `Topos`/`GrothendieckTopos` is not a dedicated structure in Mathlib.
